scope: Deterministic human-facing color labels derived from raw MoonCat hue fields for search, filtering, and display. This is not a replacement for hueInt, hueName, pale, genesis, on-chain traits, palettes, RGB values, or rendering output.

candidateSchemes.description: Use the raw snapshot hueName directly.
candidateSchemes.decision: Retain as a source field and compare it where useful, but do not make its compact vocabulary the derived display policy.
limitations: skyblue is a compact source value while the selected display label is Sky Blue.
black and white are special non-degree values rather than circular hue buckets.

candidateSchemes.description: Use twelve [0,30), [30,60), and subsequent unshifted 30-degree intervals.
candidateSchemes.decision: Rejected because the reviewed ADR defines 15-degree-shifted named boundaries instead.

candidateSchemes.description: Use the ADR's twelve equal 30-degree labels after integer hue truncation, represented as exact half-open integer intervals.
Classify source-backed Genesis black and white sentinels before circular hue bucketing.

normalHueClassification.intervalConvention: start-inclusive, end-exclusive integer intervals after modulo normalization
normalHueClassification.sourceBoundaryTranslation: ADR lower-exclusive/upper-inclusive integer boundaries are represented equivalently as [previousUpper+1, nextUpper+1).

specialClassification.precedence: Evaluate genesis marker and exact hueInt/hueName sentinel pair before normal hue modulo handling.
specialClassification.unresolvedSentinelPolicy: A 1000/2000 hueInt without the corresponding Genesis marker and source hueName remains unresolved; do not force it through modulo hue classification.

pale.reason: The source-backed pale field is preserved, but reviewed material does not justify a derived rendering or palette adjustment in this scheme.

paletteOrientation.reason: This scheme does not claim normal/inverted palette orientation or rendering behavior.

unresolvedQuestions: Whether a future reviewed renderer/palette pass should add an independently sourced palette-orientation field; this policy intentionally leaves it not classified.
Whether a full-population generated artifact should expose labels as stored rows, build-time output, or a query-time calculation; the current decision is limited to the 64-row prototype.
How a future source should classify inconsistent non-Genesis 1000/2000 sentinel combinations if they occur; this version leaves them unresolved.
limitations: The labels are human-facing derived metadata, not canonical on-chain traits or rarity categories.
This scheme does not provide RGB, hex, palette, mapColors, or rendered-image proof.
The 64-row prototype is representative only and does not establish full-population coverage or current upstream freshness.
Unknown or inconsistent special-sentinel combinations remain unresolved rather than being guessed.
